fields.Notes: Born ~1811; co-owned Otsego St. cooperage w/ H.W. Pearce; opened the saloon May 26, 1861; died in poverty, age 69, Feb 1880. Veterans reportedly offered to cover his debts (see saloon-anecdote-tracker.md, VERIFIED institutional facts).

Taylor (1913) p.207 ('Annie B. Grover'); Fair Record (1863) p.5 ('Anna Grover')

Milwaukee Journal Sentinel, Feb. 17, 1884, p.4: 'Mrs. Priscilla B. Charles, who died in Washington, Lancaster county, Pa., a few days ago, was a volunteer assistant in the famous Cooper Shop Volunteer refreshment saloon in Philadelphia. It was there she first met her husband, a member of the 121st Pennsylvania volunteers.' Found 2026-07-16.
fields.Notes: 'Charles' is her married surname -- likely taken from her husband, the unnamed 121st PA Volunteers member she met while volunteering at the saloon. Maiden name not given in this source. She died shortly before Feb 17, 1884 in Washington (borough), Lancaster County, PA -- worth checking Lancaster County vital/cemetery records if her maiden name or husband's full name is ever needed. A genuine wartime-romance story tied to the saloon, distinct from the other named volunteers/committee members already in this table.

fields.Sources: Taylor (1913) p.207-208 ('Dr. Elias Ward'); Fair Record (1863) p.5 ('W. Eliab Ward') and p.14 ('Dr. Eliab Ward')
fields.Notes: Served throughout the war per Taylor. Name spelling genuinely inconsistent across both sources -- do not assume which is correct without further research.
fields.Best Full Name: Elias Ward or Eliab Ward -- spelling not resolved

fields.Sources: Taylor (1913) p.207, 212 (source for the Cooper Shop reopening/closing detail); Fair Record (1863) p.5
fields.Notes: Also the addressee (with C.V. Fort) on the Gale letter re: the Cooper Shop (see saloon-anecdote-tracker.md, Report 6). Possibly authored Lossing correspondence and a memorial broadside per Report 9 (unconfirmed lead).

fields.Sources: Taylor (1913) p.207 (full name 'Sarah L. Holland'); Fair Record (1863) p.5 ('Sarah Holland')
fields.Notes: Named on the HSP printed-slip committee list alongside 'Miss R. Sickles' as a candidate for the UVRS diarist's identity. Confirmed as a real 1861 committee member by two independent primary sources (2026-07-15). Sickles does NOT appear in either roster -- Taylor notes a supplementary May 1864 General Committee report (not reproduced) that might cover her; not yet located.

fields.Sources: Taylor (1913) p.214 (Soldiers' Home board); House Divided (GAR Post 94 naming); prior research sessions (Cooper Shop hospital role)
fields.Notes: Died of overwork at the Refreshment Saloon per Taylor and House Divided; no specific death date confirmed in either. A Gemini report's claim of 'December 22, 1863' as her death date was REJECTED as a likely conflation with the Cooper Shop Soldiers' Home's real opening date (see saloon-anecdote-tracker.md). GAR Post 94 named for her; a city park in the 43rd Ward also bears her name; buried at Monument Cemetery. NOTE: does not appear in Taylor's Cooper Shop 1861 committee roster itself -- her documented hospital role may postdate that snapshot, or come from a different source not yet cross-checked here.
fields.Role/Title: Cooper Shop hospital leadership (per prior sessions' research); Vice-President, Soldiers' Home in the City of Philadelphia (per Taylor)
